page: Amazon Web Services i4i.large is a storage-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro System. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The instance provides 2 virtual CPUs mapped to a single physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 468 GB NVMe SSD, while baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate moderate single-core performance alongside constrained multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU test yielded an average score of 1880.58, and the Geekbench single-core score reached 1558.0. However, multi-core performance is limited, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 1923.48 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 1806.0, reflecting the single physical core constraint. Memory bandwidth is also low, measuring 14.18 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 13.19 GB/sec for large-block write operations.

Given the high local NVMe SSD storage capacity relative to its compute footprint, the i4i.large is designed for storage-intensive workloads that require low-latency local access but do not demand intensive multi-core processing. It is suitable for small-scale databases, NoSQL stores, and caching layers. However, the instance exhibits weak performance in database operations with a 999.24 score on Passmark and Redis SET operations at 2.04 million operations per second, indicating that CPU and memory bandwidth bottlenecks may limit throughput during high-concurrency database tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services i4i.large is a storage-optimized instance utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8375C processor. Operating at 3.5 GHz, this x86_64 instance provides 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core, 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a bundled 468 GB NVMe SSD. Network performance is anchored by a 1 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1558.0, but multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are low due to the single-core hardware constraint. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring fast local NVMe storage without heavy parallel compute requirements. It is best suited for small-scale databases, local caching, and lightweight transactional applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.large is a storage-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8375C processor, 2 vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a bundled 468 GB NVMe SSD for low-latency local storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.large is an Intel-powered, storage-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 468 GB NVMe SSD.
tagline: A storage-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for low-latency workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz
Bundled 468 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Suited for small-scale databases and caching layers
